entities.name: Player
entities.notes: THE PLAYER: a dynamic circle body (2D physics in the x/y plane). The input-bindings map A/D to move_x and Space to jump; the control system accelerates toward move_x * max-run-speed and applies the jump impulse when grounded -- the same per-tick logic scripts/movement.ts authors in TypeScript. Its landings open the particle puff; its jumps and the coin pickup fire the authored audio events.

entities.name: Ground
entities.notes: Static box, top face at y == 0 (half-extents (24, 1) in the runtime mirror -- schema v1 transforms carry position only; collider sizes/materials live in the mirror).

entities.name: Platform-A
entities.notes: Static platform (box half-extents (2, 1) in the mirror). The Barrel drops onto it.

entities.name: Platform-B
entities.notes: A second static platform higher up the left tower.

entities.name: Crate
entities.notes: The pushable crate: a dynamic body (mass 2) resting on the ground in the player's run path -- running into it shoves it along (the smoke gate asserts it moved). Its collider is a CIRCLE of radius 1 in the runtime mirror (the classic 2D round-collider-under-a-crate-sprite trick): the physics2d v1 narrow phase resolves circle-circle/circle-box only, so a box collider could not rest on the static box ground; swap to a box collider when the package grows box-box contacts.

entities.name: Barrel
entities.notes: A free dynamic circle dropped over Platform-A, so the platform geometry is genuinely load-bearing in the sim (not decorative).

entities.name: Coin
entities.notes: The pickup: not a physics body -- the gameplay system collects it when the player overlaps its position, firing the coin audio event once.

entities.name: LandingPuff
entities.notes: The landing particle emitter anchor: rate 0 at rest; each player landing repositions it to the player and opens the rate for a short deterministic puff.

instances.notes: One instance of the room; per-instance state lives in `overrides` below.

notes: The startup scene: a small but REAL 2D platformer level (M6 EXIT). It instances the shared
room sub-scene (L-35), adds a camera, and lays out the game entities: the input-driven Player,
the Ground and two Platforms, the pushable Crate, the Barrel that exercises Platform-A, the
Coin pickup, and the LandingPuff emitter anchor. Authored positions are INTEGERS (exactly
representable in Q16 fixed-point) -- the runtime scenario mirror registered as session scenario
'platformer-2d' (src/tests/integration/games/) parses THIS file for the entity layout, so
renaming or moving an entity fails the game-smoke-platformer-2d ctest (rot-proof beyond schema
validity). Collider sizes, masses, the anim-graph rig, and the fixed per-tick system order
(input -> control -> physics2d -> gameplay -> particles -> animation) live in that mirror; the
player's per-tick control logic is ALSO authored in TypeScript (scripts/movement.ts).
The `overrides` array retargets the instanced torch: an id-path override with a recorded
`base` so `context query --overrides diverged` can flag drift (R-CLI-006).
